reasoning: A blockbuster US movie (budget >= $100M) opening before the end of 2025 would need to have been written in 2023 or 2024. Following the 2023 WGA strike, major studios (AMPTP) agreed to strict restrictions preventing AI from writing or rewriting literary material, and AI generation cannot be considered source material or diminish a writer's credit. All $100M+ US productions essentially require union labor and are subject to these rules. Additionally, acknowledging profound AI involvement in the script of a massive tentpole film would invite overwhelmingly negative PR and backlash from creatives and audiences. Given the long production timelines of massive budget features and the regulatory/PR environment established by the 2023 WGA strike, it is extremely unlikely any such film will be released and acknowledged as AI-written by the end of 2025.
